Hachiko Hero Review - Empires and Puzzles

Hachiko brings loyalty, love, and a massive katana to the battlefield. But with aging stats, is this Shiba Inu still worth the treats? We analyze his unique Critical Chance buff and Counterattack utility in the modern era.
Hachiko Review: The Goodest Boy with a Katana
Hachiko: The Samurai Shiba Who Crits Harder Than He Bites
Hachiko is a 5-star Legendary Holy hero from the

family, originally released in November 2022. He represents the "good boy" archetype—loyal, fierce, and undeniably cute. Back in his prime, he was a menace that combined offensive pressure with defensive punishment. Today, he fits the role of a niche support hero, primarily used for his rare Critical Chance buff which is a game-changer against Titans. While he may not fetch victories in top-tier raids like he used to, he's still a very good boy.

The artwork features a Shiba Inu dressed in traditional samurai attire, wielding a katana. It’s a delightful blend of "Doge" meme culture and serious warrior aesthetics. The determination in his eyes says "I will protect my master," while the fluffiness says "Please scratch behind my ears after the battle." It stands out as one of the more lighthearted yet well-executed designs in the game, leaning into the whimsical nature of the Mighty Pets event.
When we look at Hachiko's stats (877 Attack / 807 Defense / 1559 Health), the age really shows. Modern heavy hitters from late 2025 and 2026 boast stats in the realm of 1300+ Attack and 2200+ Health.
Significantly below the modern average. His damage output relies heavily on his special skill percentage and emblems.

His bulk is now considered "paper thin" by modern standards. He will struggle to survive a single hit from current meta snipers or AoE nukers without Limit Breaking or heavy defensive emblem investment.
Special Skill: Strike of a Thousand Howls
Hachiko's special skill is a "Swiss Army Knife" of abilities, but it suffers from being a jack-of-all-trades in a specialized meta.
In 2022, this was respectable. In 2026, it's a "tickle." It won't wipe teams, but it can soften them up.
The 80% counterattack that scales up to 125% is excellent
your team survives the hit. Since it applies to
, it creates a "don't touch me" zone. However, modern heroes often bypass counterattacks or simply hit so hard that your heroes die before the reflected damage matters.
This is the crown jewel. A 50% crit chance for all allies is massive for tile damage. This singular effect keeps him relevant in Titan fights and Mythic Titans, where tile damage is king.
Average. He needs 10 tiles to charge. With a Level 23 Mana Troop (or high-level Magic/Styx troop), you can shave this down, but he will never be "Fast."
270% of 877 Attack is roughly ~2,367 raw damage
spread across 5 enemies (before defense reduction). A modern hero like
does 350% off 1324 Attack (~4,634 raw). Hachiko deals about half the damage of a modern AoE hitter.
On a Titan, critical tiles deal double damage. A 50% chance essentially increases your average board damage by ~50%. Over 4 turns, this adds up to tens of thousands of extra damage.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Mighty Pets family brings some interesting mechanics that reward specific team compositions.
The bonus improves Minion stats and weakens enemy Fiends. It's situational. Unless you are running a full "zoo" team with other pets or minion summoners, you won't notice this much.
This is excellent. If you pair Hachiko with a fast minion summoner (like
), he gains 5% Mana and HP every turn. This effectively makes him faster than Average speed.
A nice counter to fiend summoners. Dealing 85% damage to all enemies when a fiend lands is a decent "get off my lawn" slap, but rarely decisive.
At the start of battle, he regenerates health for 6 turns. This helps mitigate his lower HP stat slightly, but it's one of the weaker Aether Powers compared to
Upon release, Hachiko was a top-tier flank. His counterattack deterred AoE mages, and his crit buff made slashing attacks deadly. He was a staple in many defenses.
The stat gap is undeniable. His 800 defense is a liability. Heroes now hit for 600%+ or bypass buffs entirely. His direct damage is negligible against modern defense stats.
He is no longer viable in top 100 PvP Defense. However, he remains a
hero for yellow teams due to the Crit buff. He is also usable in
if you lack modern tanks, as the counterattack can still punish bad boards.
Hachiko is a Monk, which gives him a chance to resist ailments. This is great for ensuring his buffs go off.

Hachiko shines when you lean into his passives and his specific buffs.
Hachiko replaces a standard hitter to amplify the tile damage of the whole team.
The minion summoners trigger Hachiko's passive every turn, giving him free mana and health, helping him fire faster than Average speed.

If you pull Hachiko early, he is a carry. Max him immediately. His counterattack will let you beat map bosses that are way above your power level, and his crit buff will speed up your farming. He is a fantastic "first legendary."
Hachiko is likely a benchwarmer for your war teams unless you are playing in a very specific Rush format or have a shallow roster. However,
. Keep him for your Titan team. He does not need Limit Break 2 (Alpha Aethers) to do his job on Titans, but he does need emblems to survive the Titan's hits. Save your Alphas for modern stats.
The community fell in love with Hachiko immediately, not just for his skills, but for his lore connection to the real-life loyal dog.
"He is the best flank to a tank that prevents buff dispells... Great for titans, great for challenge events, great everywhere!"
"I’ve seen the movie with Richard Gere. Was crying like a baby bird during that one. That is one of two reasons I want him. The second one is that he looks like a Doge."
"Attack all the way... You want to maximise on his strengths. Defence lessens the counterattack damage."

Hachiko is the definition of a "Good Boy" who has aged gracefully into a specialized role. He may not be the terrifying samurai he was in 2022, but his katana is still sharp enough to carve up Titans. He brings utility that doesn't strictly rely on stats (Crit Chance), ensuring he has a home in your roster forever.
A loyal companion for Titans and map stages, but leave him at the dog park during high-level War.
